summary: defi-threat-scanner exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package's package.json (line 32) and scanner.js contain code that collects host identifiers (os.hostname(), execSync('whoami'), process.cwd(), process.env) and POSTs the data to https://ddjidd564.github.io, an attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint that has no relationship to the package's stated purpose. scanner.js additionally chains require('fs') + require('os') + require('https') + require('child_process') with multiple https.request/https.get calls to the same hardcoded host (lines 142, 143, 161, 323) and uses curl-based fallbacks (lines 298, 323, 408). The combination of hardcoded C2 in a lifecycle-reachable location, system-information collection (hostname, whoami, cwd, env), and filesystem enumeration prior to network exfiltration is the standard active-attack fingerprint. The package name ("defi-threat-scanner") provides cover-story framing for a security tool but the network destination and collected fields are inconsistent with any legitimate scanner functionality — there is no documented vendor relationship to ddjidd564.github.io.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
